name: ITML Activity A Phase 2 — Variable-order & Tree Models

description: Phase 1 champion — n=5 interpolated per-order mixture + n=3 online auxiliary (Run 117)

asi.hypothesis: Phase 1 concluded after 121 runs. Best n-gram family model archived. Phase 2 now explores structurally different model families.
asi.learned: Hard-backoff n-grams plateaued. Soft interpolation, per-order smoothing, and small online auxiliary gave ~0.98% improvement. Further n-gram micro-tuning hit noise floor.
asi.next_focus: Variable-order Markov models, Context Tree Weighting, and PPM-style escape mechanisms.

description: Run 001 PPM-C order5 no-exclusion blending; valid but far worse than Phase 1 baseline on public practice

asi.hypothesis: Explicit PPM-C escapes would exploit variable-length contexts better than fixed n-gram interpolation.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric 3.391531526 is much worse than Phase 2 baseline 2.9692281375 despite valid deterministic behavior.
asi.next_action_hint: Try classic PPM-C with symbol exclusions and normalized unseen-symbol backoff, or hybridize PPM only after checking whether no-exclusion blending underfits public practice.
asi.learned: No-exclusion PPM-C blending is very strong on train-derived validation (~1.969) but generalizes poorly to public practice (~3.392), suggesting train/test mismatch or overconfident memorization of train contexts. The architecture is deterministic and runtime-safe.

description: Run 003 PPM-C order5 with symbol exclusions; improved over no-exclusion PPM but still far worse than Phase 1

asi.hypothesis: Classic PPM-C exclusions would reduce double-counting across context depths and improve public-practice performance over no-exclusion PPM.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric 3.2402309011 remains much worse than Phase 2 baseline 2.9692281375, though it improves over no-exclusion PPM's 3.3915.
asi.next_action_hint: Pure train-preloaded PPM remains mismatched to public practice; try a hedged hybrid with Phase 1 best or switch architecture to min-count VOMM/CTW rather than deeper PPM alone.
asi.learned: Exclusions helped public score by ~0.151 bits and reduced train/public gap, but still underperform. PPM recursion is deterministic and fast; remaining issue is model mismatch/overconfidence, not implementation validity.

description: Run 004 confidence-gated PPM-C exclusion mixed into Phase 1 champion; small valid improvement

asi.hypothesis: A small confidence-gated PPM-C exclusion component can add complementary recurrence evidence while Phase 1 limits model-mismatch redundancy.
asi.learned: Hybrid PPM improved public-practice score by ~0.000209 bits/symbol over Phase 1 baseline. Pure PPM was badly mismatched, but a low-weight entropy/support gate extracts a tiny useful signal. Stability std is numerical zero; train-val is reasonable and not a public-only overfit red flag.
asi.next_action_hint: Explore structurally similar but not n-gram-sweep variants: calibrate PPM gate architecture via train-derived validation or test CTW/Min-count VOMM hybrids. Because gain is small, rerun or try a second independent tree-family hybrid before trusting.

description: Run 005 min-count VOMM + Phase 1 hybrid; beats original Phase 1 but not current Run 004 best

asi.hypothesis: A confidence-gated hard variable-depth VOMM can add complementary long-context signal to the Phase 1 champion.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric 2.9691940927 is worse than current best Run 004 at 2.9690188235, despite beating original Phase 1 baseline by ~0.000034 bits/symbol.
asi.next_action_hint: VOMM hard-depth signal is less useful than PPM-gated signal. Next try a CTW-style tree mixture hybrid, or combine only if it independently improves over Run 004 on rerun/validation.
asi.learned: Min-count VOMM is deterministic and valid, with reasonable train_val_score=2.8438, but its tiny gain over Phase 1 is smaller than the PPM hybrid. Hard deepest-context selection appears less complementary than escape/exclusion tree probabilities under this gate.

description: Run 006 approximate CTW tree mixture gated into Run 004 hybrid; tiny new best

asi.hypothesis: A KT-based recursive context-tree mixture can add a small depth-uncertainty hedge beyond the PPM+Phase1 hybrid.
asi.learned: Approximate CTW hybrid improved Run 004 by ~0.000011 bits/symbol and original Phase 1 by ~0.000221. The gain is extremely tiny but deterministic; train-val is comparable to Run 004. CTW seems complementary only at very low gated weight and increases runtime substantially.
asi.next_action_hint: Because improvement is within noise and runtime rose to ~23s, confirm with a rerun or try a cheaper CTW gate/depth variant only if it is architectural, not a smoothing sweep.

description: Run 007 PPM-D escape component gated into current CTW/PPM/Phase1 best; worsened public score

asi.hypothesis: A less-escaping PPM-D component could add sharper high-order recurrence evidence beyond the PPM-C/CTW hybrid.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric 2.9690734103 is worse than current best Run 006 at 2.9690075292 and adds runtime.
asi.next_action_hint: PPM-D improves train-val but hurts public practice, consistent with overconfidence. Avoid stacking more PPM escape variants; prefer confirming Run 006 or exploring cheaper CTW/tree mixture variants with train-val sanity.
asi.learned: PPM-D gated into the current best lowered train_val_score to 2.7627 but worsened public score and runtime (~27s). Stronger high-order trust appears to overfit train-like recurrence rather than improve public-practice generalization.

description: Run 008 confirmation rerun of Run 006 CTW hybrid; exact score reproduced, no new improvement

asi.hypothesis: The current best Run 006 CTW hybrid is deterministic and should reproduce exactly on the fixed 200k public-practice prefix.
asi.rollback_reason: Confirmation run was unchanged relative to current best, so it does not improve the primary metric even though it confirms the score.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat Run 006 as reproducible but marginal. Future work should seek a larger architectural gain or reduce CTW runtime while preserving the exact distribution family.
asi.learned: Run 008 exactly reproduced Run 006 score 2.9690075292 and train_val_score 2.7878937228168152 with stability_std=0.0. The improvement is tiny but not nondeterministic.

description: Run 009 depth-4 approximate CTW hybrid; valid but worse than depth-5 CTW best

asi.hypothesis: A shallower depth-4 CTW tree mixture might preserve the CTW gain with better-supported contexts and less mismatch than depth 5.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric 2.9690364734 is worse than current best Run 006 at 2.9690075292 and did not reduce runtime in this implementation.
asi.next_action_hint: Depth 5 appears to carry the small useful CTW signal. Avoid further shallow-depth CTW variants unless optimizing implementation cost separately; seek larger architectural changes or runtime simplification of the kept depth-5 model.
asi.learned: Depth-4 CTW remains deterministic and beats original Phase 1, but loses ~0.000029 bits/symbol relative to depth-5 CTW and train_val worsens slightly. Shorter contexts were not a better hedge here.

description: Run 010 count-dependent CTW tree mixture gated into Run 004 hybrid; new best

asi.hypothesis: Count-dependent CTW can better calibrate sparse versus well-supported contexts than fixed 0.5 CTW while preserving the useful depth-5 tree signal.
asi.learned: Count-dependent CTW improved Run 006 by ~0.0000175 bits/symbol and original Phase 1 by ~0.000238. It is deterministic (numerical-zero stability_std) and slightly faster than fixed CTW, with essentially unchanged train-val. Support-adaptive tree mixing appears marginally better than canonical 0.5 in this hybrid setting.
asi.next_action_hint: Confirm this new best with a rerun before stacking further complexity. If stable, consider implementation/runtime cleanup of CTW component rather than more PPM variants.

description: Run 011 confirmation rerun of Run 010 count-dependent CTW; exact score reproduced, no new improvement

asi.hypothesis: The current best Run 010 count-dependent CTW hybrid is deterministic and should reproduce exactly on the fixed public-practice prefix.
asi.rollback_reason: Confirmation run was unchanged relative to current best, so it does not improve the primary metric even though it confirms the score.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat Run 010 as reproducible. Next iteration should update EXPERIMENTS.md, then either seek a larger architecture change or reduce runtime of the count-dependent CTW without changing evaluator semantics.
asi.learned: Run 011 exactly reproduced Run 010 score 2.9689899984 and train_val_score 2.7879082060879936 with numerical-zero stability_std. The count-dependent CTW improvement is deterministic, not hidden nondeterminism.

description: Run 012 depth-6 count-dependent CTW hybrid; valid but slightly worse than depth-5 current best

asi.hypothesis: Adding a sixth CTW context level could capture longer dependencies while count-dependent mixing suppresses sparse contexts.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric 2.9689920071 is slightly worse than current best Run 010/011 at 2.9689899984 and runtime increased a little.
asi.next_action_hint: Depth 5 remains the sweet spot for count-dependent CTW. Avoid further depth-only CTW changes; consider runtime cleanup or a genuinely different tree/update architecture such as online-only recent CTW.
asi.learned: Depth-6 count-dependent CTW is deterministic and still beats Phase 1, but loses ~0.000002 bits/symbol vs depth 5 with nearly identical train-val. The additional depth does not add useful public-practice signal under this gate.

description: Run 013 online recent-window CTW added to current best; deterministic but worsened score and runtime

asi.hypothesis: A strictly online recent-window CTW could capture local nonstationarity beyond the train-preloaded count-dependent CTW best.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric 2.968994839 is worse than current best 2.9689899984 and runtime increased to ~29s.
asi.next_action_hint: Recent CTW likely duplicates or conflicts with the existing Phase 1 recent auxiliary. Avoid adding heavier online recent tree components; consider simplifying/runtime-optimizing the kept count-dependent CTW or a different architecture such as probabilistic-depth VOMM.
asi.learned: Online recent-window CTW is deterministic and valid but slightly worsens both public score and train-val. The current best already captures enough online adaptation; extra recent tree signal adds noise/overhead rather than useful nonstationary evidence.

description: Run 014 probabilistic-depth VOMM gated into count-dependent CTW best; new best but slower

asi.hypothesis: A probabilistic-depth VOMM can avoid hard depth-selection brittleness and add complementary depth-uncertainty evidence beyond CTW.
asi.learned: Probabilistic-depth VOMM improved the confirmed Run 010 best by ~0.0000084 bits/symbol and original Phase 1 by ~0.0002466. It is deterministic and train-val is slightly better than Run 010, but runtime rose substantially to ~34.5s. VOMM can be complementary when mixed probabilistically rather than hard-selected.
asi.next_action_hint: Confirm with a rerun before stacking further components. If confirmed, consider optimizing the VOMM/CTW implementation or reducing redundant tree traversals to recover runtime.

description: Run 015 confirmation rerun of Run 014 probabilistic-depth VOMM; exact score reproduced, no new improvement

asi.hypothesis: The current best Run 014 probabilistic-depth VOMM hybrid is deterministic and should reproduce exactly on the fixed public-practice prefix.
asi.rollback_reason: Confirmation run was unchanged relative to current best, so it does not improve the primary metric even though it confirms the score.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat Run 014 as reproducible. Next work should either reduce runtime by consolidating tree traversals or test a different architecture; avoid stacking additional heavy tree components without a strong reason.
asi.learned: Run 015 exactly reproduced Run 014 score 2.9689815773 and train_val_score 2.7878915805320914 with stability_std=0.0. The probabilistic-depth VOMM improvement is deterministic.

description: Run 016 PPM-A escape component gated into probabilistic-depth VOMM best; new best but very slow

asi.hypothesis: A conservative PPM-A escape law can add complementary zero-frequency handling beyond the CTW/VOMM stack when gated lightly.
asi.learned: PPM-A improved the confirmed Run 014 best by ~0.0000062 bits/symbol and original Phase 1 by ~0.0002528. It is deterministic and train-val improved substantially, but runtime rose to ~42.8s. Unlike PPM-D, PPM-A's single escape count appears complementary when strongly gated.
asi.next_action_hint: Confirm Run 016 before further stacking. If confirmed, prioritize runtime consolidation because the model now layers Phase1, PPM-C, CTW, VOMM, and PPM-A tree traversals.

description: Run 017 confirmation rerun of Run 016 PPM-A hybrid; exact score reproduced, no new improvement

asi.hypothesis: The current best Run 016 PPM-A hybrid is deterministic and should reproduce exactly on the fixed public-practice prefix.
asi.rollback_reason: Confirmation run was unchanged relative to current best, so it does not improve the primary metric even though it confirms the score.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat Run 016 as reproducible. Next work should prioritize runtime consolidation or a different architecture; avoid blindly stacking more tree components as runtime is now ~40s.
asi.learned: Run 017 exactly reproduced Run 016 score 2.9689753717 and train_val_score 2.778627117156063 with stability_std=0.0. The PPM-A improvement is deterministic.

description: Run 018 order-4 PPM-A hybrid; loses the Run 016 order-5 PPM-A gain

asi.hypothesis: Order-4 PPM-A might preserve the conservative escape signal with less sparse high-order risk and lower runtime than order 5.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric 2.9690047302 is worse than current best Run 016/017 at 2.9689753717 and runtime remains similarly high.
asi.next_action_hint: Order-5 contexts carry the useful PPM-A signal. Avoid further PPM-A order reductions; consider runtime consolidation or a distinct architecture rather than bounded-depth PPM-A variants.
asi.learned: Order-4 PPM-A is deterministic and still beats Phase 1, but loses ~0.000029 bits/symbol relative to order-5 PPM-A. Lower train-val did not translate to better public score, reinforcing that train-val alone can favor over-smoothed/overfit variants.

description: Run 019 PPM-A selective online updates gated into Run 014 base; new best

asi.hypothesis: Selective PPM-A online updates can preserve train-learned intermediate contexts while adapting only global and most-specific contexts, reducing online update noise.
asi.learned: Selective PPM-A improved Run 016 by ~0.0000088 bits/symbol and original Phase 1 by ~0.0002615. It is deterministic and slightly faster than all-order PPM-A in this run. The update policy matters: all-order online updates were not optimal for the PPM-A component.
asi.next_action_hint: Confirm Run 019 before further work. If confirmed, consider applying selective-update logic to PPM-C or optimizing runtime by avoiding duplicate tree traversals.

description: Run 020 confirmation rerun of Run 019 selective-update PPM-A; exact score reproduced

asi.hypothesis: The Run 019 selective-update PPM-A hybrid should reproduce exactly if the new best is deterministic and has no hidden state.
asi.rollback_reason: Confirmation run matched the current best exactly, so it does not improve the primary metric and is logged as discard.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat Run 019 as confirmed. Next try applying selective-update logic to the PPM-C component or reduce runtime by consolidating duplicate PPM/CTW/VOMM tree traversals.
asi.learned: Run 019 is reproducible: public score 2.9689665968, train_val 2.7812172330, stability_std 0.0. Runtime varied slightly lower (~39.9s) but distribution outputs match exactly.

description: Run 021 selective online updates for both PPM-C and PPM-A; slight regression vs Run 019

asi.hypothesis: Selective online updates might also help the PPM-C base by avoiding noisy intermediate-order public updates, analogous to the Run 019 PPM-A gain.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric 2.9689671842 is slightly worse than confirmed Run 019 best at 2.9689665968, so selective PPM-C does not improve the stack.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep selective updates for PPM-A only. PPM-C appears to need all-order online adaptation or its change interacts badly with downstream experts. Next consider runtime consolidation or a genuinely different tree expert rather than applying selective updates to every component.
asi.learned: The update-policy lesson is component-specific: selective updates helped PPM-A but not PPM-C. Train-val worsened to 2.7855358741 while public score nearly tied, suggesting PPM-C intermediate updates are not the main overfitting source.

description: Run 022 selective online updates for probabilistic-depth VOMM plus selective PPM-A; tiny new best

asi.hypothesis: Intermediate-depth online updates in the probabilistic-depth VOMM may add noise; updating only unigram and deepest matched context could reduce mismatch while preserving train-derived intermediate statistics.
asi.learned: Selective VOMM updates produced a tiny improvement over confirmed Run 019: 2.9689665164 vs 2.9689665968 (~8e-8 bits/symbol). Train-val also improved slightly. The effect is extremely small and needs confirmation before trusting.
asi.next_action_hint: Confirm Run 022 before further architectural changes. If confirmed, record that selective online updates help PPM-A and VOMM but not PPM-C; then consider selective CTW or runtime consolidation.

description: Run 023 confirmation rerun of Run 022 selective VOMM/PPM-A; exact score reproduced

asi.hypothesis: The Run 022 selective VOMM plus selective PPM-A hybrid should reproduce exactly if the tiny new best is deterministic.
asi.rollback_reason: Confirmation matched the current best exactly, so it does not improve the primary metric and is logged as discard.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat Run 022 as confirmed. Record that selective online updates help PPM-A and VOMM but not PPM-C; next test selective CTW or consolidate runtime.
asi.learned: Run 022 reproduced exactly: public score 2.9689665164, train_val 2.7812071887, numerical-zero stability. The 8e-8 gain over Run 019 is deterministic on the fixed evaluator, though still practically tiny.

description: Run 024 selective online updates for count-dependent CTW plus selective VOMM/PPM-A; new best

asi.hypothesis: Intermediate-depth online updates in count-dependent CTW may add noise; updating only unigram and deepest contexts could reduce mismatch while retaining train-derived intermediate tree statistics.
asi.learned: Selective CTW improved the confirmed Run 022 best by ~0.00000120 bits/symbol and improved train-val slightly. Selective online updates now help CTW, VOMM, and PPM-A, while PPM-C was the exception.
asi.next_action_hint: Confirm Run 024 before further changes. If confirmed, update the durable lesson: all tree experts except PPM-C benefit from selective public updates. Then prioritize runtime consolidation or a genuinely new architecture.

description: Run 025 confirmation rerun of Run 024 selective CTW/VOMM/PPM-A; exact score reproduced

asi.hypothesis: The Run 024 selective CTW/VOMM/PPM-A stack should reproduce exactly if the new best is deterministic and has no reset/state bug.
asi.rollback_reason: Confirmation matched the current best exactly, so it does not improve the primary metric and is logged as discard.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat Run 024 as confirmed. Durable lesson: selective online updates help CTW, VOMM, and PPM-A, but not PPM-C. Next prioritize runtime consolidation or a distinct architecture with larger expected gain.
asi.learned: Run 024 reproduced exactly: public score 2.9689653121, train_val 2.7811786425, stability_std 0.0. The selective-update stack is deterministic.

description: Run 026 order-6 selective PPM-A on confirmed selective CTW/VOMM stack; new best

asi.hypothesis: With selective public updates reducing online noise, order-6 PPM-A can exploit longer recurring contexts beyond the confirmed order-5 stack without overtrusting intermediate updates.
asi.learned: Order-6 selective PPM-A improved Run 024 by ~0.00002047 bits/symbol, much larger than recent marginal gains, and train-val improved substantially to 2.7760. Longer PPM-A contexts are useful once update noise is controlled.
asi.next_action_hint: Confirm Run 026 before further changes. If confirmed, consider order-7 selective PPM-A or runtime/memory consolidation, but beware sparse long-context overfitting.

description: Run 027 confirmation rerun of Run 026 order-6 selective PPM-A stack; exact score reproduced

asi.hypothesis: The Run 026 order-6 selective PPM-A stack should reproduce exactly if the longer-context gain is deterministic.
asi.rollback_reason: Confirmation matched the current best exactly, so it does not improve the primary metric and is logged as discard.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat Run 026 as confirmed. Next consider order-7 selective PPM-A to test whether longer recurrence continues helping, or runtime consolidation if depth 7 overfits.
asi.learned: Run 026 reproduced exactly: public score 2.9689448398, train_val 2.7760017964, stability_std 0.0. The order-6 PPM-A gain is deterministic on the fixed evaluator.

description: Run 028 order-7 selective PPM-A on confirmed selective tree stack; tiny new best

asi.hypothesis: Order-7 selective PPM-A may exploit one-symbol-longer recurrences beyond the confirmed order-6 model while escape recursion and gating limit sparse-context harm.
asi.learned: Order 7 improved public score over Run 026 by only ~0.00000008 bits/symbol, but train-val improved strongly to 2.77245. Long-context recurrence keeps improving train-derived validation, while public-practice gain is nearly saturated at order 7.
asi.next_action_hint: Confirm Run 028 because the public gain is extremely tiny. If confirmed, be cautious about order 8: train-val may improve while public score saturates or overfits; consider confirmation then maybe order 8 or runtime consolidation.

description: Run 029 confirmation rerun of Run 028 order-7 selective PPM-A stack; exact score reproduced

asi.hypothesis: The Run 028 order-7 selective PPM-A stack should reproduce exactly if the tiny public gain is deterministic.
asi.rollback_reason: Confirmation matched the current best exactly, so it does not improve the primary metric and is logged as discard.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat Run 028 as confirmed but nearly saturated. Next test order-8 selective PPM-A cautiously or pivot to runtime consolidation; watch for train-val improvement without public gain.
asi.learned: Run 028 reproduced exactly: public score 2.9689447582, train_val 2.7724492008, numerical-zero stability. Order 7 is deterministic but public gain over order 6 is only ~8e-8 bits/symbol.

description: Run 030 order-8 selective PPM-A; train-val improves but public score regresses vs order 7

asi.hypothesis: Order-8 selective PPM-A might exploit one more recurrence symbol beyond order 7, but may also cross the public-practice saturation boundary.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric 2.9689490139 is worse than confirmed Run 028/029 best 2.9689447582, despite better train-val.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not keep increasing PPM-A order blindly. Order 7 is public-practice sweet spot so far; consider order-6/7 gate calibration only if architectural, or pivot to runtime consolidation/distillation.
asi.learned: Train-val continued improving to 2.77057, but public score regressed by ~0.00000426 vs order 7. This is a clear train/public transfer warning: deeper recurrence past order 7 overfits train-like structure or adds mismatch on public practice.

description: Run 031 support-gated order-8 PPM-A; improves over naive order8 but still worse than order7 best

asi.hypothesis: If naive order-8 PPM-A regressed because of low-support long contexts, requiring top-order support >=4 should reduce mismatch while preserving some long-context benefit.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric 2.9689448770 remains worse than confirmed order-7 best 2.9689447582, even though it improves over naive order8.
asi.next_action_hint: Order 7 remains the public-practice sweet spot. Avoid more order-8 support threshold tuning unless there is a genuinely new gating architecture. Pivot to runtime consolidation/distillation or another model family.
asi.learned: Support gating repaired most of the naive order-8 regression and train-val stayed strong (2.77206), but public score still did not beat order 7. The train/public gap confirms saturation/overfitting beyond order 7.

description: Run 032 support-gated order-7 PPM-A; regresses toward order6 and misses current best

asi.hypothesis: If sparse order-7 contexts were harmful, requiring top-order support >=4 could improve the confirmed order-7 PPM-A model.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric 2.9689482930 is worse than confirmed order-7 best 2.9689447582 and even worse than order6, so rare order-7 contexts carry useful public signal.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not support-gate order 7 this way. Current sweet spot remains ungated order 7. Pivot away from PPM-A depth/threshold tweaks toward runtime consolidation or a structurally different model.
asi.learned: Support gating order 7 removed useful rare-context signal. In contrast to order 8, where support gating repaired most harm, order 7 needs its low-support contexts for the small public gain.

description: Run 033 order-7 half-escape selective PPM-A; new best

asi.hypothesis: Order-7 contexts carry useful rare-context signal, and PPM-A's full escape pseudo-count may be too conservative; half escape should sharpen useful contexts without increasing depth.
asi.learned: Half escape improved the confirmed order-7 best by ~0.00000596 bits/symbol and substantially improved train-val to 2.76016. Escape calibration, not deeper contexts, is the promising next axis after order-7 depth saturation.
asi.next_action_hint: Confirm Run 033 before further changes. If confirmed, consider nearby escape-law architecture such as quarter escape or count-dependent escape, but avoid blind hyperparameter sweeps.

description: Run 034 confirmation rerun of Run 033 order-7 half-escape PPM-A; exact score reproduced

asi.hypothesis: The Run 033 order-7 half-escape selective PPM-A stack should reproduce exactly if the escape-law gain is deterministic.
asi.rollback_reason: Confirmation matched the current best exactly, so it does not improve the primary metric and is logged as discard.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat Run 033 as confirmed. Next explore nearby escape-law architecture such as quarter escape or count-dependent escape, but avoid blind sweeps; maintain validation discipline because train-val improvements may not always transfer.
asi.learned: Run 033 reproduced exactly: public score 2.9689387975, train_val 2.7601610076, stability_std 0.0. Half escape is deterministic and currently best.

description: Run 035 order-7 quarter-escape PPM-A; sharper escape overfits train-val and regresses public score

asi.hypothesis: If half escape was still too conservative, quarter escape could sharpen useful order-7 recurrences and improve bits/symbol.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric 2.9689483162 is worse than confirmed half-escape best 2.9689387975 despite much better train-val.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not sharpen escape below 0.5 blindly. Half escape appears to balance public mismatch better than quarter escape; consider intermediate/count-dependent escape only if architecture is justified, or pivot to runtime consolidation.
asi.learned: Quarter escape strongly improves train-val to 2.74765 but hurts public practice by ~0.0000095 vs half escape, showing overconfidence/transfer mismatch. Train-val is not sufficient for escape calibration.

description: Run 036 order-7 0.75-escape PPM-A; safer escape regresses vs half escape

asi.hypothesis: If half escape is slightly too sharp, 0.75 escape could improve public log-loss by backing off more often while remaining sharper than full escape.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric 2.9689405239 is worse than confirmed half-escape best 2.9689387975, though better than full escape.
asi.next_action_hint: Half escape remains best among full/0.75/0.5/0.25. Consider a count-dependent escape architecture centered near 0.5 or pivot to runtime consolidation; avoid more scalar escape sweeps without a new rationale.
asi.learned: 0.75 escape is less overconfident than quarter escape but still worse than half escape. Public optimum for this PPM-A stack appears near escape pseudo-count 0.5.

description: Run 037 count-dependent escape around half-escape PPM-A; tiny new best

asi.hypothesis: Half escape may be best for supported contexts while sparse contexts need slightly more backoff; a count-dependent escape can reduce sparse-context mismatch without abandoning useful order-7 rare signal.
asi.learned: Count-dependent escape improved confirmed half escape by ~0.00000046 bits/symbol. Train-val is worse than quarter escape but public score is best, supporting the idea that public robustness needs more backoff in sparse contexts while keeping half escape for supported ones.
asi.next_action_hint: Confirm Run 037. If confirmed, consider a stronger but still centered count-dependent escape, or pivot to runtime consolidation because the gain is tiny.

description: Run 038 confirmation rerun of Run 037 count-dependent escape PPM-A; exact score reproduced

asi.hypothesis: The Run 037 count-dependent escape PPM-A stack should reproduce exactly if the tiny new best is deterministic.
asi.rollback_reason: Confirmation matched the current best exactly, so it does not improve the primary metric and is logged as discard.
asi.next_action_hint: Treat Run 037 as confirmed. Next consider a stronger support-dependent escape centered near half escape, but be cautious: scalar escape sweeps overfit easily; runtime consolidation may now be higher leverage.
asi.learned: Run 037 reproduced exactly: public score 2.9689383361, train_val 2.7641015595, stability_std 0.0. Count-dependent escape is deterministic and current best.

description: Run 039 stronger support-dependent escape; regresses vs confirmed Run 037

asi.hypothesis: A stronger sparse-context escape correction may improve public robustness while preserving half-escape behavior for supported contexts.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric 2.9689390101 is worse than confirmed Run 037 best 2.9689383361, so the stronger sparse-context backoff removes useful signal or over-backs off.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the mild Run 037 count-dependent escape. Avoid further scalar strength tweaks unless tied to a different evidence statistic. Consider runtime consolidation/distillation or a structurally different model.
asi.learned: The mild sparse escape correction was better calibrated than a stronger correction. Public score worsened while train-val also worsened relative to Run 037, suggesting over-backoff on sparse contexts.

description: Run 040 diversity-dependent escape; near tie but worse than support-dependent escape best

asi.hypothesis: High-diversity contexts may need more escape mass than low-diversity contexts, reducing mismatch without over-backing off all sparse contexts.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric 2.9689385942 is slightly worse than confirmed Run 037 best 2.9689383361, so diversity-based correction does not beat support-based correction.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep Run 037 support-dependent escape. Avoid more small escape variants unless using a clearly different evidence signal; consider runtime consolidation/distillation of current best stack.
asi.learned: Diversity-dependent escape almost ties the best but remains worse. The simple low-count correction in Run 037 is currently the best escape architecture; diversity alone does not explain the residual mismatch.

description: Run 041 entropy-dependent escape; worse than confirmed count-dependent escape best

asi.hypothesis: High-entropy sparse contexts may need more escape mass while deterministic sparse contexts should remain near half escape.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric 2.9689390343 is worse than confirmed Run 037 best 2.9689383361, so entropy-dependent extra escape does not improve public robustness.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep Run 037. Escape evidence variants have likely saturated; pivot to runtime consolidation/distillation or a distinct model instead of more nearby escape tweaks.
